_note: Seed legal-citation dataset. Pointers to real authorities; not legal advice. Extend at <project>/.tool/safety/local_legal.json (same schema) to overlay project- or jurisdiction-specific entries.

entries.summary: DEA schedules controlled substances by abuse potential and accepted medical use. Manufacture, distribution, and possession outside an active DEA registration are federal offenses; state schedules may add to but not subtract from federal.

Manufacture, storage, and transport of explosive materials require ATF licenses (manufacturer, dealer, user) and adherence to magazine construction and recordkeeping standards. Possession without a license is a federal offense.

NFA-regulated items (suppressors, short-barreled rifles/shotguns, machine guns, destructive devices, AOWs) require ATF Form 4 approval plus a $200 tax stamp before transfer; manufacture requires Form 1 approval in advance.

Source, byproduct, and special nuclear material are regulated by the NRC (or an Agreement State). Quantity-of-concern thresholds drive licensing, transport (49 C.F.R. 173), and security (Increased Controls) requirements.

Generators of listed or characteristic hazardous waste must classify, manifest, and dispose through permitted TSDFs. Small-quantity (<100 kg/mo) and very-small-quantity generator thresholds determine recordkeeping burden.

Small UAS (<55 lb) operated for any non-recreational purpose requires a Part 107 Remote Pilot certificate, airworthiness compliance, registration, and Remote ID broadcast. Waivers cover BVLOS, night, and operations over people.
